mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains above 5.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which leads to short-term positive market momentum as earnings revisions turn positive within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during periods of rising industrial production, or the signal reverses direction during supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 235.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening to combat inflation, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation readings, or the threshold fails to predict negative returns when wage growth remains subdued.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative when payroll growth outpaces unemployment declines.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion and profit margin improvement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings catch up to macro data.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the correlation between GDP growth and equity returns turns negative during stagflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting economic activity and weakening manufacturing demand, prompting risk-off positioning and negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Markets remain flat or positive during consecutive industrial production declines, or the signal fails to predict downturns when government stimulus offsets private sector contraction.
